treatment: Publication identity, agency disclaimer, high-consequence scope, and page-number convention were manually paraphrased. Photographs, illustrations, acknowledgments detail, and third-party expressive matter were excluded.
reason: Identity and limitations are needed to interpret the technical digest.

treatment: Retained system thinking, competent-lead requirement, fail-safe planning, ratings, and the warning that a manual does not replace training. Design-factor values remain explicitly trail-contextual.
reason: High-value foundational safety reasoning with clear source scope.

treatment: No wire-rope construction, clipping, splicing, or retirement procedure was retained.
reason: Material-specific industrial procedures require their own standards and training and are not central to the requested fiber-rope knowledge.

treatment: Retained construction, contextual material traits, product-specific selection, MBS/WLL distinction, knot and bend effects, handling, inspection, logging, and retirement. Omitted universalized knot percentages, a source-specific broken-fiber threshold, overload heuristics, and heat-finishing procedure.
reason: Dense transferable rope knowledge; unsafe generalizations were removed.

treatment: No chain grading, repair, or use procedure was retained.
reason: Specialized lifting-chain practice is outside this bounded fiber-rope corpus.

treatment: Retained configuration dependence, identification, edge protection, center-of-gravity, load-sharing, inspection, and manufacturer-control principles. Images and construction-specific tables were excluded.
reason: Transferable attachment and geometry knowledge, with no human-suspension certification claim.

treatment: Product operation, reeving, and field-use procedures were not retained.
reason: Tool-specific operation belongs with manufacturer instructions and supervised training.

treatment: Retained block, sheave, groove, D/d, connector orientation, marking, WLL/MBS, rigging-plate, friction, and compatibility principles. Numerical side-load and sheave heuristics are labeled source-specific. Product promotion, diagrams, and anchor procedures were excluded.
reason: High-value mechanics and component-selection reasoning.

treatment: Tool descriptions and field procedures were not retained.
reason: Low relevance to the requested corpus and dependent on product-specific training.

treatment: Retained force direction, static/dynamic/shock distinctions, work, mechanical advantage, friction, center of gravity, and smooth-loading principles. Broad dynamic and shock ranges are warnings, not design factors.
reason: Core transferable mechanics.

treatment: Retained site survey, load-path sketch, equipment list, role, braking, environmental, and failure-consequence planning.
reason: High-value process knowledge that reduces hidden assumptions.

source_section: Chapter 11: Anchors, Spars, and Artificial Spars
treatment: Retained uncertainty, substrate, direction, unequal sharing, movement, bending moment, and professional-evaluation principles. Excluded soil-anchor, deadman, rock, tree, stump, spar, A-frame, tripod, and guying construction recipes.
reason: Mechanics are useful, but trail-field recipes must not be transferred to human suspension, trees, or indoor hardpoints.

source_section: Chapter 12: Inspection of the Rigging System
treatment: Retained two-pass system tracing, component orientation, WLL checks, communication, fly-zone analysis, gradual test loading, and post-unload inspection. Product anecdotes were excluded.
reason: Strong system-level inspection and consequence-control material.

treatment: Detailed skidding, high-lead, skyline, cableway, carriage, and related layouts were not retained.
reason: Trail-material configurations are specialized and could be misapplied as improvised human-support instructions.

treatment: Retained only the dependence of control and heat on rope, device, wraps, speed, contamination, and manufacturer instructions. Product-specific lowering procedures were excluded.
reason: The governing friction principle is useful; operational recipes are device-specific.

treatment: Retained lead responsibility, universal stop authority, closed-loop communication, exclusion zones, escape routes, gear quarantine, hazard analysis, emergency readiness, briefing, and after-action review. Agency forms and personal-data detail were excluded.
reason: High-value safety-management knowledge.

treatment: Retained system complexity, force visualization, rated-component discipline, limits of knowledge, and professional consultation. The decorative third-party quotation was excluded.
reason: Concise reinforcement of the source's governing principles.

treatment: Only terms needed to understand retained material were paraphrased: force, block, sheave, bight, fly zone, mechanical advantage, rigging, sling, thimble, vector, MBS, WLL, and related acronyms. Logging- and wire-specific vocabulary was excluded.
reason: Definitions improve technical coherence without teaching title or URL trivia.

treatment: Bibliographic listings, URLs, addresses, phone numbers, vendor descriptions, and product sources were not retained.
reason: Avoids URL memorization, promotion, and third-party material outside the bounded digest.

source_section: Appendix A: Terminating and Managing Wire Rope
treatment: Wire-rope cutting, seizing, clip placement, spacing, torque, efficiency, and source tables were not retained.
reason: Industrial wire-rope procedures require current manufacturer instructions and material-specific training.

treatment: Retained the principle of recording identity, manufacture and service dates, construction, dimensions, MBS, use, inspections, possible damage, and condition. The form layout was excluded.
reason: Lifecycle records support inspection but do not replace it.

treatment: Log-weight formulas and trail-material density tables were not retained.
reason: Low relevance to the requested rope-mechanics corpus and easy to misuse outside their assumptions.

source_section: Appendices D-E: force and vector calculations
treatment: Retained trigonometric identities, symmetric sling and suspended-line equations, shallow-angle tension, redirect resultants, components, bending moment, assumptions, worked values, and the source's false-confidence warning. Rebuilt one numeric table from equations; excluded figures. Explicitly corrected page 128, 133, and 149 typographical inconsistencies and flagged ambiguous captions.
reason: Exceptionally useful mechanics when assumptions, limitations, and errata remain attached.

treatment: Detailed calculations not needed by the retained lessons, references, product sources, vendor material, and expressive back matter were excluded.
reason: Avoids promotion, URL memorization, and material that was not independently rights-cleared or needed for the digest.

source_section: All photographs, illustrations, and expressive source tables
treatment: No image or figure was retained. Numerical relationships were independently restated only where supported by text, equations, and reproducible arithmetic.
reason: Eligible federal text is treated as presumptively public domain, but embedded or contributed expressive components are not blanket-cleared.
